★ ISSUE 07 · RISING MILLIONAIRE PREVIEW

How did they close RM 1.89 million on one deal?

Rising Millionaire preview inside. Plus a Week 1 check-in on your new website features.

This coming Monday at PropNex HQ, Alex Ng & Joe Chia walk you through the industrial case that paid out RM 1.89 million — the handling, the strategy, the mindset. Watch the 90-second preview inside. Plus today's Insider Edge shows you 3 moves to turn today's Real Estate Closing Masterclass notes into content that becomes your resource. And 1 week after launch, here's how to actually use each of the 3 new website features.

A Note to Agents

Five minutes here. Pick what helps. Run it this week.

★ INSIDER EDGE · ISSUE 07

Today, you learn from Yong Hock. Here’s how to turn those notes into content that builds your name.

Real Estate Closing Master Class is today. Most agents will walk out with a notebook full of gold — and let it sit there. This week, 3 moves to turn today’s learning into content that becomes your resource.

The Insider Edge
The Insider Edge

✦ 把課變成你的內容

上完課,筆記通常會怎樣?

今天 Closing Master Class 之後,三步讓筆記不再只是筆記。

✦ Turn today's class into content

What usually happens to your class notes?

Three moves to turn today's Closing Masterclass into content people actually see.

Swipe
PropNex Malaysia
01
The Insider Edge 1 / 3

一次只講一個重點

十個重點想分享,還是忍住,只挑一個。

大腦一次吸收的資訊很有限。塞十個,一個都記不住;挑一個講深,反而黏得住。

今天 KEO 講了很多,只挑「closing 之前先了解客戶心態」一個,光這個就夠寫一篇。

One Point at a Time

You'll want to say ten things. Say one.

Your brain only takes in so much at once. Pack in ten, nothing sticks. Pick one and go deep, it sticks.

Yong Hock might cover ten things today. Take just one, "understand your client's mindset first," that alone is a whole post.

PropNex Malaysia
02
The Insider Edge 2 / 3

轉換成自己的語言

照抄永遠是別人的,詮釋才是你的。

先問自己:這句話為什麼打中你?然後用你的話重新講一遍。同一句,加上你的角度,就變成你的內容。

講師說「懂客戶心態」,你的詮釋:那是真的搞懂他要什麼,不是急著把 unit 推給他。

Make It Your Own

A quote is always someone else's. Your take makes it yours.

First ask: why did it hit you? Then say it your way. Same line, your angle, and now it's yours.

Trainer says "understand your client's mindset." Your take: dig into what they need, don't rush to push whatever unit you have.

PropNex Malaysia
03
The Insider Edge 3 / 3

套用自己的經歷

當下體悟 + 後來應用,都寫出來。

空談理論沒人信。你真實實作過的細節,才讓人知道你是會做的人,不是只懂理論的人。

分兩段寫:上完課當下的體悟 + 你拿去用的結果。→

Bring In Your Own Story

Write what hit you, and what happened when you used it.

Theory alone reads flat. Real detail from actually doing it is what tells people you're someone who does, not just someone who talks.

Write two beats: the moment in the room + what came after you tried it. →

PropNex Malaysia
The Insider Edge

今天公司有一場 Real Estate Closing Master Class,新加坡的 KEO Yong Hock 親自主講,還有三位 millionaire 上台分享。你會帶著一整本 closing 的乾貨走出來。

但我們每次都看到同一件事,筆記寫得很認真,然後就一直躺在筆記本裡面。

那這一次,我們想分享的方法,叫「重新推導法」。它的核心邏輯很簡單:同一個知識,如果你能用自己的話重新講一遍,會比你抄一遍記得更牢、也更久。而且當別人看到你把一件事轉化成自己的話,這就變成你的內容了,他聽到的是你的想法。

這不是憑感覺說的。早在 1978 年,兩位心理學家 Slamecka 跟 Graf 就做過實驗:一組人自己把答案想出來、用自己的話講,另一組人只是讀過、抄一遍,結果自己「生成」出來的那一組,記得明顯更牢。後來幾十年、累積到 86 個研究的統合分析,都一再驗證同一件事。這個現象,叫生成效應(Generation Effect)。

1. 一次只講一個重點

你可能有很多話想說,但還是請你忍住,只挑一個重點講。

為什麼一個內容只講一個重點?很多人會想把全部東西都塞進去,生怕漏掉哪一個就虧了。但人的大腦,處理資訊的能力其實很有限,你想講的東西很多,聽的人未必吸收得了,這跟記性好不好沒關係,是大腦的習慣。你可以回想一下,唸書或上課的時候,一堆資訊一次攤在你面前,你的第一個反應通常是抓不到重點;但如果把其他都拿走,只放一個在你面前,你的注意力自然就落在它身上。

所以聽到很多重點想分享,把它們拆成好幾篇,一次只講深一個。就像我們每週的 Insider Edge,也都盡量只挑一個重點,再把它延伸講深。

例:今天 KEO 講了很多,你只挑「closing 之前先了解客戶的心態」這一個,然後分享這個重點為什麼對你這麼重要,光這一個就夠寫一篇了。

2. 轉換成自己的語言

當你引用別人的一段話,先問問自己:這段話為什麼觸動你、為什麼讓你留下印象?

為什麼要轉換成自己的話?因為照抄一段話,它永遠是別人的;但當你加上自己的詮釋,它才變成你的內容,別人也才聽得到你的想法。方法就是,先消化這段話為什麼打中你,然後用你自己的想法,把它重新講一遍。

比如你今天聽到「AI 時代來了,要趕快學 AI」。你認同要學,但你的詮釋可以再往下走一層:其實心態比工具更重要,因為如果只是被潮流追著跑,你會一直很焦慮;不如先想清楚自己的生活要往哪走,再把工具套進來。同一句話,因為你加了自己的看法,它就變成你的話了。

例:講師說 closing rate 要先了解客戶的心態。你的詮釋可以是:了解心態,是真的去搞懂他要的是什麼,而不是急著把手上的 unit 推給他。有些人會先全部丟出去、看哪個會中;但我不認同,那只會讓客戶覺得你沒誠意,只是想趕快把東西銷掉。

3. 套用自己的經歷

上完課當下的體悟,跟後來真的拿去用的結果,都寫出來。

為什麼一定要套自己的經歷?因為這會讓人看到你真實的一面,而不是拿著理論在空談。它讓人知道你是一個會去實作的人,不是一個只懂理論的人。你可以分兩段寫,上完課的當下,跟後來的應用。

當下你可以寫你的體悟:我以前以為,把所有 unit 都攤給客戶看就好了,但其實客戶感受到的,不是我在幫他,是我急著想把東西賣給他,而不是真的了解他要什麼。

例:後來我把它用在一個卡了三個月的 case 上,先放下急著推銷的心,改成去問、去聽他到底在擔心什麼。結果就在上完課那個禮拜,我跟他談成了。

當你學會把聽到的東西變成自己的內容,這些內容,就會變成你的資源。公司的活動、公司的課程,本來就是你的素材。當你把它們轉化過、再分享出去,它就是一則很有力量的內容。它會讓人看見你是誰、認識你是誰,進而認識你背後的靠山、你的背景、你的公司,然後因為這些資源而相信你。

下一期我們聊的是,當一場典禮在你面前:如果你不是上台的那一個,你可以怎麼把它變成你的內容?如果你就是上台的那一個,你又可以怎麼在那個時刻,把自己的品牌立起來?下週見。

Today there's a Real Estate Closing Master Class. Yong Hock, our KEO from Singapore, is leading it himself, with three of our millionaires up there sharing too. You'll walk out with a notebook full of closing gold.

And here's what we see happen every time. The notes get written down, carefully, and then they just sit in that notebook. For good.

So this time we want to share something we call re-derivation. The idea is simple: when you take what you learned and say it again in your own words, you hold onto it far better, and far longer, than if you just copied it down. There's a bonus, too. The moment people see you put an idea into your own words, it stops being someone else's idea and starts being yours. They're hearing you think.

And this isn't just a nice-sounding theory. Back in 1978, two psychologists, Slamecka and Graf, ran an experiment. One group worked out the answers and said them their own way. The other just read and copied. The group that generated the answers themselves remembered them noticeably better. Decades of research since, eighty-six studies pulled into a single review, keep landing on the same spot. It even has a name. The generation effect.

1. One Point at a Time

You'll have a lot you want to say. Hold back, and pick just one.

So why keep it to a single point? Because most people try to cram everything in, scared that leaving something out means leaving value on the table. But your brain can only take in so much at once. Think back to studying for an exam: the second a wall of information lands in front of you, you can't find the point at all. Clear everything else away, leave one thing sitting there, and suddenly your attention has somewhere to land.

So when you walk out with ten takeaways, don't force them into one post. Break them up and go deep on one at a time. It's the same thing we do with The Insider Edge every week, one point, then we take it further.

E.g. Yong Hock might cover ten things today. Take just one, "before you close, understand where your client's head is at," then share why that one hit you so hard. That alone is a whole post.

2. Make It Your Own

Before you share someone's line, ask yourself why it stuck with you.

Why put it in your own words at all? Because a quote left as a quote always belongs to whoever said it. The moment you add how you read it, it becomes yours, and people get to hear how you think. So figure out why it hit you first, then say it again your way.

Say you hear "the AI era is here, better learn AI fast." You agree you should learn. But your take can go a layer deeper: mindset matters more than the tool. Chase every trend and you'll just live anxious. Get clear on where you want your life to go first, then bring the tools in. Same sentence, but the moment you add your angle, it's yours now.

E.g. The trainer says closing starts with understanding your client's mindset. Your take: that means actually digging into what they need, not rushing to push whatever unit you've got on hand. Some people throw everything at the wall to see what sticks. I don't buy it, that just tells the client you're not sincere, you're only there to move product.

3. Bring In Your Own Story

Write what hit you in the room, and what happened when you actually used it.

Why does your own experience matter so much? Because it shows people the real you, not someone reciting theory into the void. It tells them you're someone who actually goes and does it. Write it in two parts: the moment in the room, and what came after.

In the room, you might write: I used to think my job was to put every unit in front of the client. But what they felt wasn't me helping them, it was me rushing to sell, instead of really getting what they needed.

E.g. Later I tried it on a deal that had been stuck for three months. I dropped the urge to push, and just asked, and listened, to what he was actually worried about. That same week, the week of the class, we closed.

Once you can turn what you hear into your own content, that content becomes your resource. The company events, the courses, they were always your raw material. Run them through your own voice and put them out there, and you've got something with real weight behind it. People see who you are, they get to know you, and through you they start to see what's behind you, your backing, your background, your company, and they trust you because of it.

Next time: when there's a ceremony happening right in front of you. If you're not the one on stage, how do you turn it into your content? And if you are up there, how do you use that moment to build your own brand? See you next week.

PropNex Malaysia · Marketing
● WEEK 1 · CHECK-IN

1 week in — are you using them yet?

We launched the 3 website features last week. Here’s how to actually use each one this week.

FEATURE 01 · LISTING PLATFORM

Stop searching everywhere for a listing.

Every PropNex listing — searchable in one place, with filters.

Open the PropNex website. Tap Find Property. Pick Buy, Rent, or Auction. Filter by state, city, and property type — landed, condo, commercial, whatever your client wants. Sort by newest or price. Done.

Try the search now →
FEATURE 02 · YOUR EXCLUSIVE E-NAME CARDSenior Leaders First

Know exactly which event brought you the leads.

One link per event — see which one actually brought you the leads.

What it does Instead of sharing the same E-Name Card link everywhere, you can create a different link for every event you attend.

Why it matters Each link tracks its own views, contacts saved, and top channels. So you finally know if Roadshow KL brought you 20 saves, or if the one you skipped would’ve been better.

How PropNex X → E-Name Card → Manage links → New link → name it after the event → save → share the QR at the event.

1. Dashboard
2. Manage links
3. Name your event
Available now on PropNex X
FEATURE 03 · TESTIMONIAL & RATING

We’re letting your reputation show up before you do.

Let every client rate you 5-star — and become a 5-star negotiator.

Every closed deal now earns a real client review and star rating on your public PropNex profile. New leads see your track record and quietly decide to work with you. Turn every close into public proof, on autopilot.

Available in your Agent Portal

Real Estate Closing Master Class — From Zero to Your First Million

The right strategy doesn’t just teach you to sell. It changes who you become.

Success in real estate leaves clues. This is a must-attend session conducted by Lim Yong Hock — the sales strategist behind Southeast Asia’s largest listed real estate company — who shows the path from zero to first million through the eyes of Karen Tho, Anthony Chua and Anderson Yoong, as they share the decisions and choices that built their seven-figure careers.

What you can expect:

  • The proven methods and activities that drive consistent results and accelerate growth.
  • How top performers overcome challenges, build momentum, and stay focused on their goals.
  • The mindset, environment, and networks that create long-term success in real estate.

Who should attend:

  • Agents chasing their first seven-figure milestone
  • Existing agents looking for breakthrough strategies
  • Real estate pros committed to accelerating their growth

Update Your GTA Today — Stay Visible

Your GTA tells the company where you actively serve, so we can connect you with the right opportunities, projects, training, and market updates relevant to your area.

Pick both your Location and your Specializations (Residential / Commercial / Industrial / Land); leads route to the agent on file. Agents who keep their GTA updated are first in line for local visibility.
